name: KSU ASCEND Program
summary: ASCEND supports KSU students with foster care or homelessness experience through campus-based retention help.
eligibility: KSU students with foster care or homelessness experience.
address: Kennesaw State University, Kennesaw and Marietta, Georgia

name: KSU CARE Services
summary: KSU CARE Services helps students facing housing, food, and emergency basic-needs barriers.
eligibility: KSU students needing basic-needs or short-term crisis support.
address: Kennesaw State University, Kennesaw and Marietta, Georgia

name: Embark Georgia Campus Programs
summary: Embark Georgia connects students with campus support programs, scholarships, and college persistence help.
eligibility: Georgia students with foster care or homelessness experience seeking postsecondary support.

name: Embark Georgia - Chafee ETV
summary: Embark Georgia administers Chafee ETV funding for Georgia youth with qualifying foster care history.
eligibility: Youth ages 18 to 25 with qualifying foster care history and education or training plans.

name: Connected By 21
summary: Connected By 21 organizes Georgia supports for youth aging out, including housing, life skills, and transition planning.
eligibility: Georgia youth transitioning from foster care who need post-18 support planning.

name: Georgia DFCS Youth Information
summary: Georgia DFCS Youth Information highlights transition resources, youth programs, and foster-care planning supports.
eligibility: Georgia youth in care or transitioning out of care.

name: Georgia CREW
summary: Georgia CREW helps youth in care build work readiness, career exposure, and employment skills.
eligibility: Georgia youth in foster care, generally ages 14 to 21, seeking workforce preparation.

name: SNAP Works for Former Foster Youth
summary: SNAP Works can connect eligible former foster youth to training, credentials, and employment support.
eligibility: Former foster youth who meet SNAP Works program criteria in participating areas.

name: Wellroot Family Services
summary: Wellroot supports youth transitioning from foster care with housing-focused and wraparound services.
eligibility: Transition-age youth needing housing support, especially youth leaving foster care.

name: 211 Georgia
summary: 211 Georgia connects people to local housing, food, crisis, health, and community services.
eligibility: Anyone in Georgia needing help finding community resources.

name: Georgia DFCS Emergency Support
summary: Georgia DFCS provides emergency child welfare and foster-care contact pathways for urgent family or youth safety needs.
eligibility: Georgia residents needing urgent child welfare or foster-care support.

name: Georgia Medicaid Extension for Former Foster Youth
summary: Georgia extends Medicaid for many youth who were in foster care at age 18.
eligibility: Youth who were in foster care at age 18 may qualify for health coverage through age 26.

name: Extended Youth Support Services (EYSS)
summary: EYSS is part of Georgia's extended foster-care support pathway for eligible youth who aged out at 18.
eligibility: Youth who aged out at 18 and meet Georgia extended foster-care participation rules.

name: Cobb Homeless Alliance
summary: Cobb Homeless Alliance coordinates housing providers and homelessness response resources in Cobb County.
eligibility: Cobb County residents experiencing homelessness or housing instability.

name: MUST Ministries
summary: MUST Ministries offers emergency assistance, shelter-linked support, food, and basic-needs help in metro Atlanta.
eligibility: People in crisis needing food, housing support, or basic-needs assistance.

name: Atlanta Legal Aid - Cobb County
summary: Atlanta Legal Aid's Cobb office helps eligible residents with housing, family, and public-benefits legal issues.
eligibility: Low-income Cobb County residents with qualifying civil legal needs.

name: Georgia Legal Services Program
summary: Georgia Legal Services Program provides civil legal help to eligible Georgians outside metro Atlanta coverage areas.
eligibility: Low-income Georgians with qualifying civil legal problems.

name: Georgia Gateway
summary: Georgia Gateway is the main online portal for Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, and other state-administered benefits.
eligibility: Georgia residents applying for public benefits or managing an existing benefits case.
